interface_address6.description: A 127 bit IPV6 prefix from within the virtual_subnet6 prefix space with the address being the first IP of the subnet and not same as the address of virtual_subnet6. Eg if virtual_subnet6 is 2606:54c1:7:0:a9fe:12d2::/127 , interface_address6 could be 2606:54c1:7:0:a9fe:12d2:1:200/127

extra_prefixes.description: Prefixes in this list will be advertised to the customer device, in addition to the routes in the Magic routing table.

md5_key.description:
MD5 key to use for session authentication.

Note that *this is not a security measure*. MD5 is not a valid security mechanism, and the
key is not treated as a secret value. This is *only* supported for preventing
misconfiguration, not for defending against malicious attacks.

The MD5 key, if set, must be of non-zero length and consist only of the following types of
character:

* ASCII alphanumerics: `[a-zA-Z0-9]`
* Special characters in the set `'!@#$%^&*()+[]{}<>/.,;:_-~`= \|`

In other words, MD5 keys may contain any printable ASCII character aside from newline (0x0A),
quotation mark (`"`), vertical tab (0x0B), carriage return (0x0D), tab (0x09), form feed
(0x0C), and the question mark (`?`). Requests specifying an MD5 key with one or more of
these disallowed characters will be rejected.

prefix.description: A valid CIDR notation representing an IP range.

ha_link.description: mark true to use this LAN for HA probing. only works for site with HA turned on. only one LAN can be set as the ha_link.

is_prioritized.description: mark true to use this LAN for source-based prioritized traffic

vlan_tag.description: VLAN ID. Use zero for untagged.

magic_acl.description: Bidirectional ACL policy for network traffic within a site.

description.example: Allows local traffic between PIN pads and cash register.

forward_locally.description: The desired forwarding action for this ACL policy. If set to "false", the policy will forward traffic to Cloudflare. If set to "true", the policy will forward traffic locally on the Magic Connector. If not included in request, will default to false.

items.description: Array of allowed communication protocols between configured LANs. If no protocols are provided, all protocols are allowed.

unidirectional.description: The desired traffic direction for this ACL policy. If set to "false", the policy will allow bidirectional traffic. If set to "true", the policy will only allow traffic in one direction. If not included in request, will default to false.

vlan_tag.description: VLAN ID. Use zero for untagged.

health_check_rate.description: Magic WAN health check rate for tunnels created on this link. The default value is `mid`.

priority.description: Priority of WAN for traffic loadbalancing.

vlan_tag.description: VLAN ID. Use zero for untagged.

fqdn_id.description:
A custom IKE ID of type FQDN that may be used to identity the IPsec tunnel. The
generated IKE IDs can still be used even if this custom value is specified.

Must be of the form `<custom label>.<account ID>.custom.ipsec.cloudflare.com`.

This custom ID does not need to be unique. Two IPsec tunnels may have the same custom
fqdn_id. However, if another IPsec tunnel has the same value then the two tunnels
cannot have the same cloudflare_endpoint.

per_page.description: The maximum number of items per page.

total_count.description: The total number of items in the entire result set.

total_pages.description: The number of total pages in the entire result set.

cpu_pressure_10s.description: Percentage of time over a 10 second window that tasks were stalled

cpu_pressure_300s.description: Percentage of time over a 5 minute window that tasks were stalled

cpu_pressure_60s.description: Percentage of time over a 1 minute window that tasks were stalled

cpu_time_guest_ms.description: Time spent running a virtual CPU or guest OS (milliseconds)

cpu_time_guest_nice_ms.description: Time spent running a niced guest (milliseconds)

cpu_time_iowait_ms.description: Time spent wait for I/O to complete (milliseconds)

cpu_time_nice_ms.description: Time spent in low-priority user mode (milliseconds)

delta.description: Number of network operations applied during state transition

epsilon.description: Simulated number of network operations applied during state transition

ha_value.description: Numeric value associated with high availability state (0 = disabled, 1 = active, 2 = standby, 3 = stopped, 4 = fault)

io_pressure_full_10s.description: Percentage of time over a 10 second window that all tasks were stalled

io_pressure_full_300s.description: Percentage of time over a 5 minute window that all tasks were stalled

io_pressure_full_60s.description: Percentage of time over a 1 minute window that all tasks were stalled

io_pressure_some_10s.description: Percentage of time over a 10 second window that some tasks were stalled

io_pressure_some_300s.description: Percentage of time over a 3 minute window that some tasks were stalled

io_pressure_some_60s.description: Percentage of time over a 1 minute window that some tasks were stalled

kernel_ctxt.description: Number of context switches that the system underwent

kernel_processes_blocked.description: Number of processes blocked waiting for I/O

kernel_processes_running.description: Number of processes in runnable state

load_average_cur.description: Number of currently runnable kernel scheduling entities

load_average_max.description: Number of kernel scheduling entities that currently exist on the system

memory_active_bytes.description: Memory that has been used more recently

memory_anon_hugepages_bytes.description: Non-file backed huge pages mapped into user-space page tables

memory_anon_pages_bytes.description: Non-file backed pages mapped into user-space page tables

memory_available_bytes.description: Estimate of how much memory is available for starting new applications

memory_bounce_bytes.description: Memory used for block device bounce buffers

memory_buffers_bytes.description: Relatively temporary storage for raw disk blocks

memory_cached_bytes.description: In-memory cache for files read from the disk

memory_commit_limit_bytes.description: Total amount of memory currently available to be allocated on the system

memory_committed_as_bytes.description: Amount of memory presently allocated on the system

memory_dirty_bytes.description: Memory which is waiting to get written back to the disk

memory_free_bytes.description: The sum of LowFree and HighFree

memory_hugepages_free.description: The number of huge pages in the pool that are not yet allocated

memory_hugepages_rsvd.description: Number of huge pages for which a commitment has been made, but no allocation has yet been made

memory_hugepages_surp.description: Number of huge pages in the pool above the threshold

memory_hugepages_total.description: The size of the pool of huge pages

memory_inactive_bytes.description: Memory which has been less recently used

memory_k_reclaimable_bytes.description: Kernel allocations that the kernel will attempt to reclaim under memory pressure

memory_kernel_stack_bytes.description: Amount of memory allocated to kernel stacks

memory_mapped_bytes.description: Files which have been mapped into memory

memory_page_tables_bytes.description: Amount of memory dedicated to the lowest level of page tables

memory_per_cpu_bytes.description: Memory allocated to the per-cpu alloctor used to back per-cpu allocations

memory_pressure_full_10s.description: Percentage of time over a 10 second window that all tasks were stalled

memory_pressure_full_300s.description: Percentage of time over a 5 minute window that all tasks were stalled

memory_pressure_full_60s.description: Percentage of time over a 1 minute window that all tasks were stalled

memory_pressure_some_10s.description: Percentage of time over a 10 second window that some tasks were stalled

memory_pressure_some_300s.description: Percentage of time over a 5 minute window that some tasks were stalled

memory_pressure_some_60s.description: Percentage of time over a 1 minute window that some tasks were stalled

memory_s_reclaimable_bytes.description: Part of slab that can be reclaimed on memory pressure

memory_s_unreclaim_bytes.description: Part of slab that cannot be reclaimed on memory pressure

memory_secondary_page_tables_bytes.description: Amount of memory dedicated to the lowest level of page tables

memory_shmem_bytes.description: Amount of memory consumed by tmpfs

memory_shmem_hugepages_bytes.description: Memory used by shmem and tmpfs, allocated with huge pages

memory_shmem_pmd_mapped_bytes.description: Shared memory mapped into user space with huge pages

memory_swap_cached_bytes.description: Memory swapped out and back in while still in swap file

memory_swap_free_bytes.description: Amount of swap space that is currently unused

memory_swap_total_bytes.description: Total amount of swap space available

memory_vmalloc_chunk_bytes.description: Largest contiguous block of vmalloc area which is free

memory_vmalloc_total_bytes.description: Total size of vmalloc memory area

memory_vmalloc_used_bytes.description: Amount of vmalloc area which is used

memory_writeback_bytes.description: Memory which is actively being written back to the disk

memory_writeback_tmp_bytes.description: Memory used by FUSE for temporary writeback buffers

memory_z_swap_bytes.description: Memory consumed by the zswap backend, compressed

memory_z_swapped_bytes.description: Amount of anonymous memory stored in zswap, uncompressed

snmp_icmp_in_addr_mask_reps.description: Number of ICMP Address Mask Reply messages received

snmp_icmp_in_addr_masks.description: Number of ICMP Address Mask Request messages received

snmp_icmp_in_csum_errors.description: Number of ICMP messages received with bad checksums

snmp_icmp_in_dest_unreachs.description: Number of ICMP Destination Unreachable messages received

snmp_icmp_in_echo_reps.description: Number of ICMP Echo Reply messages received

snmp_icmp_in_echos.description: Number of ICMP Echo (request) messages received

snmp_icmp_in_errors.description: Number of ICMP messages received with ICMP-specific errors

snmp_icmp_in_parm_probs.description: Number of ICMP Parameter Problem messages received

snmp_icmp_in_redirects.description: Number of ICMP Redirect messages received

snmp_icmp_in_src_quenchs.description: Number of ICMP Source Quench messages received

snmp_icmp_in_time_excds.description: Number of ICMP Time Exceeded messages received

snmp_icmp_in_timestamp_reps.description: Number of ICMP Address Mask Request messages received

snmp_icmp_in_timestamps.description: Number of ICMP Timestamp (request) messages received

snmp_icmp_out_addr_mask_reps.description: Number of ICMP Address Mask Reply messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_addr_masks.description: Number of ICMP Address Mask Request messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_dest_unreachs.description: Number of ICMP Destination Unreachable messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_echo_reps.description: Number of ICMP Echo Reply messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_echos.description: Number of ICMP Echo (request) messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_errors.description: Number of ICMP messages which this entity did not send due to ICMP-specific errors

snmp_icmp_out_msgs.description: Number of ICMP messages attempted to send

snmp_icmp_out_parm_probs.description: Number of ICMP Parameter Problem messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_redirects.description: Number of ICMP Redirect messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_src_quenchs.description: Number of ICMP Source Quench messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_time_excds.description: Number of ICMP Time Exceeded messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_timestamp_reps.description: Number of ICMP Timestamp Reply messages sent

snmp_icmp_out_timestamps.description: Number of ICMP Timestamp (request) messages sent

snmp_ip_default_ttl.description: Default value of the Time-To-Live field of the IP header

snmp_ip_forw_datagrams.description: Number of datagrams forwarded to their final destination

snmp_ip_forwarding_enabled.description: Set when acting as an IP gateway

snmp_ip_frag_creates.description: Number of datagrams generated by fragmentation

snmp_ip_frag_fails.description: Number of datagrams discarded because fragmentation failed

snmp_ip_in_addr_errors.description: Number of input datagrams discarded due to errors in the IP address

snmp_ip_in_delivers.description: Number of input datagrams successfully delivered to IP user-protocols

snmp_ip_in_discards.description: Number of input datagrams otherwise discarded

snmp_ip_in_hdr_errors.description: Number of input datagrams discarded due to errors in the IP header

snmp_ip_in_receives.description: Number of input datagrams received from interfaces

snmp_ip_in_unknown_protos.description: Number of input datagrams discarded due unknown or unsupported protocol

snmp_ip_out_discards.description: Number of output datagrams otherwise discarded

snmp_ip_out_no_routes.description: Number of output datagrams discarded because no route matched

snmp_ip_out_requests.description: Number of datagrams supplied for transmission

snmp_ip_reasm_fails.description: Number of failures detected by the reassembly algorithm

snmp_ip_reasm_reqds.description: Number of fragments received which needed to be reassembled

snmp_ip_reasm_timeout.description: Number of seconds fragments are held while awaiting reassembly

snmp_tcp_active_opens.description: Number of times TCP transitions to SYN-SENT from CLOSED

snmp_tcp_attempt_fails.description: Number of times TCP transitions to CLOSED from SYN-SENT or SYN-RCVD, plus transitions to LISTEN from SYN-RCVD

snmp_tcp_curr_estab.description: Number of TCP connections in ESTABLISHED or CLOSE-WAIT

snmp_tcp_estab_resets.description: Number of times TCP transitions to CLOSED from ESTABLISHED or CLOSE-WAIT

snmp_tcp_in_csum_errors.description: Number of TCP segments received with checksum errors

snmp_tcp_in_errs.description: Number of TCP segments received in error

snmp_tcp_max_conn.description: Limit on the total number of TCP connections

snmp_tcp_out_rsts.description: Number of TCP segments sent with RST flag

snmp_tcp_passive_opens.description: Number of times TCP transitions to SYN-RCVD from LISTEN

snmp_tcp_rto_max.description: Maximum value permitted by a TCP implementation for the retransmission timeout (milliseconds)

snmp_tcp_rto_min.description: Minimum value permitted by a TCP implementation for the retransmission timeout (milliseconds)

snmp_udp_in_datagrams.description: Number of UDP datagrams delivered to UDP applications

snmp_udp_in_errors.description: Number of UDP datagrams failed to be delivered for reasons other than lack of application at the destination port

snmp_udp_no_ports.description: Number of UDP datagrams received for which there was not application at the destination port

system_boot_time_s.description: Boottime of the system (seconds since the Unix epoch)

t.description: Time the Snapshot was recorded (seconds since the Unix epoch)

uptime_idle_ms.description: Sum of how much time each core has spent idle

uptime_total_ms.description: Uptime of the system, including time spent in suspend

magic_app_source_subnets.description: IPv4 CIDRs to associate with traffic decisions. (IPv6 CIDRs are currently unsupported)

allOf.description: A valid CIDR notation representing an IP range.

magic_colo_regions.description: List of colo regions for the ECMP scope.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful

a.description: Time the Event was collected (seconds since the Unix epoch)

n.description: Sequence number, used to order events with the same timestamp

t.description: Time the Event was recorded (seconds since the Unix epoch)

automatic_return_routing.description: True if automatic stateful return routing should be enabled for a tunnel, false otherwise.

cloudflare_gre_endpoint.description: The IP address assigned to the Cloudflare side of the GRE tunnel.

customer_gre_endpoint.description: The IP address assigned to the customer side of the GRE tunnel.

description.description: An optional description of the GRE tunnel.

interface_address.description: A 31-bit prefix (/31 in CIDR notation) supporting two hosts, one for each side of the tunnel. Select the subnet from the following private IP space: 10.0.0.0–10.255.255.255, 172.16.0.0–172.31.255.255, 192.168.0.0–192.168.255.255.

interface_address6.description: A 127 bit IPV6 prefix from within the virtual_subnet6 prefix space with the address being the first IP of the subnet and not same as the address of virtual_subnet6. Eg if virtual_subnet6 is 2606:54c1:7:0:a9fe:12d2::/127 , interface_address6 could be 2606:54c1:7:0:a9fe:12d2:1:200/127

mtu.description: Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) in bytes for the GRE tunnel. The minimum value is 576.

name.description: The name of the tunnel. The name cannot contain spaces or special characters, must be 15 characters or less, and cannot share a name with another GRE tunnel.

ttl.description: Time To Live (TTL) in number of hops of the GRE tunnel.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful

magic_gre.description: The configuration specific to GRE interconnects.

cloudflare_endpoint.description: The IP address assigned to the Cloudflare side of the GRE tunnel created as part of the Interconnect.

health_state.description: Name of tunnel health state (unknown, healthy, degraded, down)

health_value.description: Numeric value associated with tunnel state (0 = unknown, 1 = healthy, 2 = degraded, 3 = down)

interface_name.description: The tunnel interface name (i.e. xfrm1, xfrm3.99, etc.)

probed_mtu.description: MTU as measured between the two ends of the tunnel

recent_healthy_pings.description: Number of recent healthy pings for this tunnel

recent_unhealthy_pings.description: Number of recent unhealthy pings for this tunnel

enabled.description: Determines whether to run healthchecks for a tunnel.

rate.description: How frequent the health check is run. The default value is `mid`.

target.description: The destination address in a request type health check. After the healthcheck is decapsulated at the customer end of the tunnel, the ICMP echo will be forwarded to this address. This field defaults to `customer_gre_endpoint address`. This field is ignored for bidirectional healthchecks as the interface_address (not assigned to the Cloudflare side of the tunnel) is used as the target. Must be in object form if the x-magic-new-hc-target header is set to true and string form if x-magic-new-hc-target is absent or set to false.

type.description: The type of healthcheck to run, reply or request. The default value is `reply`.

magic_app_subnets.description: IPv4 CIDRs to associate with traffic decisions. (IPv6 CIDRs are currently unsupported)

allOf.description: A valid CIDR notation representing an IP range.

magic_psk_metadata.description: The PSK metadata that includes when the PSK was generated.

last_generated_on.description: The date and time the tunnel was last modified.

operstate.description: UP/DOWN state of the network interface

speed.description: Speed of the network interface (bits per second)

magic_app_hostnames.description: FQDNs to associate with traffic decisions.

status.description: Current status of the network interface

client_id.description: Client ID of the device the IP Address was leased to

expiry_time.description: Expiry time of the DHCP lease (seconds since the Unix epoch)

hostname.description: Hostname of the device the IP Address was leased to

mac_address.description: MAC Address of the device the IP Address was leased to

magic_app_breakout_preferred_wans.description: WAN interfaces to prefer over default WANs, highest-priority first. Can only be specified for breakout rules (breakout must be true).

oneOf.description: A valid CIDR notation representing an IP range.

critical_celcius.description: Critical failure temperature of the component (degrees Celsius)

current_celcius.description: Current temperature of the component (degrees Celsius)

max_celcius.description: Maximum temperature of the component (degrees Celsius)

items.description: An IP address prefix in CIDR format.

additionalProperties.description: IP address associated with the MAC address

reservations.description: Mapping of MAC addresses to IP addresses

file_system.description: File system on disk (EXT4, NTFS, etc.)

is_read_only.description: Determines whether the disk is read-only

is_removable.description: Determines whether the disk is removable

a.description: Time the Snapshot was collected (seconds since the Unix epoch)

t.description: Time the Snapshot was recorded (seconds since the Unix epoch)

sent_carrier.description: Number of packets not sent due to carrier errors

sent_drop.description: Number of packets dropped during transmission

ip_address.description: IP address of the network interface

magic_health_check_target.description: The destination address in a request type health check. After the healthcheck is decapsulated at the customer end of the tunnel, the ICMP echo will be forwarded to this address. This field defaults to `customer_gre_endpoint address`. This field is ignored for bidirectional healthchecks as the interface_address (not assigned to the Cloudflare side of the tunnel) is used as the target.

effective.description: The effective health check target. If 'saved' is empty, then this field will be populated with the calculated default value on GET requests. Ignored in POST, PUT, and PATCH requests.

saved.description: The saved health check target. Setting the value to the empty string indicates that the calculated default value will be used.
